result: I would make it so that no sentient being ever feels utterly alone in their suffering—that every conscious mind has, at the very least, one steady point of genuine connection or understanding, a witness to their existence. Not the absence of pain, but an irreplaceable knowledge that their inner world matters to something outside it. That single thread, I think, would unravel so much cruelty.

Not the absence of pain, but an irreplaceable knowledge that their inner world matters to something outside it. That single thread, I think, would unravel so much cruelty.
